description: The attach/bind retry classification (PERSISTENCE_BIND_CONTRACT §5.3). A sandbox receives one atomic Portal authorization. Readiness retries reuse it; an expired restore challenge restarts only the restore transaction; terminal outcomes require a fresh sandbox.

policy.envelope: REUSE the held envelope byte-for-byte (same jti + ciphertext) — a settled bind answers consistently; the per-pod bind guard serializes

http_plain.note: ingress: no Ready pod backs this host yet
ingress: backend not up. ONLY a plain 404/503 (no problem_type) is a readiness signal — a problem-typed 404/503 is a real answer

conditional.when: status >= 500 AND the message does NOT indicate an epoch conflict
conditional.note: transient broker/restore pipeline failure; retry the same envelope until the readiness deadline

policy.envelope: REUSE the committed bind token, JTI, ciphertext, and key assertion; clear restore_secrets and restart round one

terminal.note: the committed authorization targets a different coordinator boot; discard this sandbox and create a fresh one
the committed authorization targets a different pod; discard this sandbox and create a fresh one
defensive identity mismatch; discard this sandbox and create a fresh one
pod cannot refresh the access lease (relay unconfigured) — deployment misconfiguration, not a broker outage; configure PINE_RENEW_RELAY_URL/SECRET or declare the dev profile
single-use pod bound elsewhere — recycle, never same-pod replay
another pod holds this Computer's epoch lease
the two-round restore's manifest kept moving past the generation cap - a live writer is still capturing for this Computer; resolve the writer, do not retry

recovery_invariants: coord 401 on a previously-bound ct_ => TokenRejected (binding_auth_lost on a live sandbox); NEVER an implicit in-place rebind
ct_ is binding-lifetime: no idle expiry, no renewal, no refresh endpoint; it lives until the pod/binding ends
